questions.question: 9 (a) Electrical power is produced in a power station by an alternating current (a.c.) generator. The output of the generator has a voltage of 22 000 V. The electrical power is transmitted at a voltage of 400 000 V. Explain why electrical power is transmitted at a voltage of 400 000 V and not 22 000 V. .......................................................
questions.text: 9 (a) Electrical power is produced in a power station by an alternating current (a.c.) generator. The output of the generator has a voltage of 22 000 V. The electrical power is transmitted at a voltage of 400 000 V. Explain why electrical power is transmitted at a voltage of 400 000 V and not 22 000 V. .......................................................

parts.text: 9 (a) Electrical power is produced in a power station by an alternating current (a.c.) generator. The output of the generator has a voltage of 22 000 V. The electrical power is transmitted at a voltage of 400 000 V. Explain why electrical power is transmitted at a voltage of 400 000 V and not 22 000 V. .......................................................
parts.answer: high voltage results in low current (for same power) B1 Any two from: • heat loss depends on current • less power / energy loss (in transmission) • thinner wires can be used B2 0625/43 October/November 2020

Efficiency = (useful) power output (total) power input C1 P = V I in any form C1 power output = (20 × 2.3) = 46 (W) C1 power input = 46 ÷ 0.9 = 51 (W) C1 input current = (51 ÷ 240=) 0.21 A A1 / coil / solenoid becomes a magnet B1 iron core / coil / solenoid attracts iron armature B1 armature pivots/moves and contacts touch / there is a current in circuit B B1
current in circuit B does not stop when switch in circuit A is opened B1 steel remains magnetised when there is no current in the coil B1 0625/43 October/November 2020
